description: ChatGPT's launch in November 2022 triggered rapid public engagement with AI capabilities and risks. Support for government regulation of AI grew from ~45% to ~70%+ within a year, accelerated by open letters, Senate hearings, and rapid capability advances.

key_events.description: ChatGPT launches November 2022, reaches 100M users in 2 months. Public fascination mixed with concern. Initial discourse focuses on capabilities more than risks.
Future of Life Institute open letter (March 2023) calls for 6-month pause on AI development. Signed by Elon Musk, Steve Wozniak, 1000+ researchers. Media amplification massive.
Sam Altman testifies before US Senate (May 2023). Calls for AI regulation himself. Bipartisan agreement on need for guardrails — rare political consensus.
AI-generated deepfakes proliferate. Hollywood strikes partly over AI. EU AI Act advances. UK hosts AI Safety Summit at Bletchley Park (November 2023). Existential risk discourse peaks.
Pew Research (2024): 72% of Americans more concerned than excited about AI. Reuters/Ipsos: 70%+ support federal regulation. Support plateaus at high level with low opposition.

calibration_notes: Rapid monotonic increase in regulation support driven by capability shock, elite warnings, and bipartisan political alignment. Pew Research tracked concern rising from ~38% (2021) to 52% (early 2023) to 72% (2024). Unusual bipartisan consensus — both left (labor/equity concerns) and right (safety/control concerns) converged on regulation support. Tests: technology shock response, elite signaling amplification, undecided-to-pro conversion, bipartisan consensus formation.
